resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Increased genetic risk of adverse reaction
resultRows.description: The public Mental Health Genetic Guide sample row appears in the antidepressants section with CYP2C19 and Swissmedic source labeling.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Moclobemide. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2D6: Normal metabolizers of atomoxetine have a lower likelihood of response as compared to poor metabolizers. This is associated with increased discontinuation due to lack of efficacy as compared to poor metabolizers.
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled CYP2D6 antidepressant/ADHD medication result with a multi-line narrative result field.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Atomoxetine. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2D6: Normal metabolism of paroxetine to less active compounds. Paroxetine-associated phenoconversion of normal metabolizers to intermediate or poor metabolizers due to CYP2D6 autoinhibition may occur and is dose-dependent and greater at steady state concentrations.
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled CYP2D6 antidepressant result for paroxetine.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Paroxetine. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2B6: Small increase in metabolism of sertraline to less active compounds when compared to CYP2B6 normal metabolizers. CYP2C19: Reduced metabolism of sertraline to less active compounds when compared to CYP2C19 normal metabolizers.
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled multi-gene antidepressant result with CYP2B6 and CYP2C19 gene columns.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Sertraline. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2C19: Reduced metabolism when compared to CYP2C19 normal metabolizers. Higher plasma concentrations may increase the probability of side effects.
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled CYP2C19 antidepressant result with probability wording that must remain sample-row wording unless calibrated local evidence is supplied.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Citalopram. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: OPRM1: Increased risk of suicidal ideation in people with major depressive disorder
resultRows.description: The sample row shows an Article-labeled OPRM1 antidepressant result; local output must not infer suicidality or current mental state.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Tianeptine. Use this as Mental Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local psychiatric diagnosis, current mental-state assessment, medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

practicalNextSteps: Review any medication questions with a clinician or pharmacist who knows the full medication and mental-health context.
Use the appendix to see which pharmacogene and clinical-context inputs were unavailable.
Do not use sample rows or derived PGx evidence alone to start, stop, switch, rank, or dose medication.

references.title: FDA Table of Pharmacogenomic Biomarkers in Drug Labeling
Mental Health Genetic Guide sample report

genotypeSummary.effect: One increased function allele and one normal function allele
One no function allele and one normal function allele
One decreased function allele and one normal function allele
Synonymous and missense variant rows are visible in the sample appendix.
Reference and intron variant rows are visible in the sample appendix.
Reference row is visible in the sample appendix.
The public sample appendix lists GRIN2B and NFKB1 as no-variant rows.
genotypeSummary.genotype: No variants found that alter gene function

probabilities.label: Calibrated personal probability
probabilities.note: The deterministic report body does not quantify medication response, adverse reaction, diagnosis, treatment response, prognosis, or dosing probabilities.

uncertainty: Sample-report rows are output-format evidence, not local clinical decisions.
Derived pharmacogene calls do not include the full medication, diagnosis, treatment, ancestry, assay, and clinician-reviewed context needed for medication decisions.
Missing inputs are listed explicitly instead of being inferred.
missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction is not available yet.

Medication list, indication, dose, treatment history, psychiatric diagnosis, symptoms, current mental state, suicidality assessment, clinician review, phenotype confidence, and medication-decision context are not supplied.
Expanded user-specific psychiatric medication PGx coverage for all sample-table genes is unavailable unless supplied by a validated local PGx caller.
limitations: This local artifact uses public sample-report structure and synthetic derived evidence for validation.
It does not assess diagnosis, symptoms, current mental state, suicidality, treatment response, medication suitability, safety, efficacy, or dosing.
It does not include private variants, full CYP2D6 copy-number resolution, validated clinical medication review, or clinician interpretation.
